en.extract:
The Banggai golden bulbul (Hypsipetes harterti) is a species of passerine bird in the bulbul family Pycnonotidae. It is endemic to Banggai Island  in Indonesia. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.
The Banggai golden bulbul was formerly considered as conspecific with   five other bulbuls, all called the northern golden bulbul before they were all split. This bulbul is now both placed with 24 other species in the genus Hypsipetes that was introduced in 1831 by Nicholas Vigors.

The '''Banggai golden bulbul''' ('''''Hypsipetes harterti''''') is a species of [[passerine]] bird in the bulbul family [[Pycnonotidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Banggai Island]]  in Indonesia. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s.

The Banggai golden bulbul was formerly considered as [[conspecific]] with   five other bulbuls, all called the [[northern golden bulbul]] before they were all split. This bulbul is now both placed with 24 other species in the genus ''[[Hypsipetes]]'' that was introduced in 1831 by [[Nicholas Aylward Vigors|Nicholas Vigors]].<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela Rasmussen | date=July 2023 | title=Bulbuls | work=IOC World Bird List Version 13.2 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/bulbuls/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=24 July 2023}}</ref><ref>{{ cite journal | last1=Collar | first1=N.J. | last2=Eaton | first2=J.A.| last3=Hutchinson | first3=R.O.  | date=2013 | title=Species limits in the Golden Bulbul ''Alophoixus'' (''Thapsinillas'') ''affinis'' complex | journal=Forktail | volume=29 | pages=19-24 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/58109296 }}</ref>
